mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market deterioration signals severe economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis liquidity injection phase signals immediate capacity utilization improvements, triggering short-term momentum buying that yields positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns (win rate < 50%) during post-crisis recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when liquidity conditions tighten.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting expectations of aggressive future tightening that suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods meeting the trigger condition, or the negative correlation vanishes (Rank IC > -0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirms genuine labor market tightening, which sustains consumer spending growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or the signal breaks down during structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real GDP growth signal immediate earnings deterioration for S&P 500 constituents, causing rapid portfolio rebalancing that produces negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth rates correlate with positive 5-day forward returns (Rank IC > 0.01) or the signal shows no predictive power across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, simultaneous growth in industrial production and retail sales indicates robust demand-pull dynamics that support equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns (win rate < 52%) during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism reverses when supply constraints dominate demand growth.
